_DOC: STUDIO — MASTER JSON Prompt Template v2.2
_DOC_canon: All rules derive from STUDIO_Production_Canon_v1.0.md. Do not deviate.
_DOC_version: v2.2 — from v2.1: @1 relabeled SETTING/ENVIRONMENT PLATE (was: BODY/CLOTHING PHYSICS REFERENCE — corrected to match Grok Imagine @ reference workflow); clothing/fabric physics moved to scene field / prose_prompt

shot_identity._DOC: Administrative header — not sent to Grok. For production filing and slate.
shot_identity.director_note: [One line: what this shot must accomplish narratively]
shot_identity.legal_gate_status: [GREEN / YELLOW / RED — must be GREEN before generation]

_DOC_command: Fixed. Always 'generate video'. Do not alter.
_DOC_scene: WHAT THIS FIELD IS: Stage direction only. Body action, position, movement, weight, surface contact.
WHAT THIS FIELD IS NOT: No camera language. No emotional adjectives. No 'and then' sequencing.
CHARACTER DIRECTION RULE (hard rule): Characters/actors — write BEHAVIORAL DIRECTION ONLY.
What are they doing. How are they moving. What is their energy/intention.
DO NOT describe appearance (hair, skin, face, body) — the editor has the 3D render reference; Grok has the casting reference.
EXAMPLE (correct): 'She moves with deliberate restraint — each step measured, her energy coiled, attention scanning the far corridor without urgency.'
EXAMPLE (wrong): 'A tall dark-haired woman with olive skin and sharp cheekbones moves through the corridor.'
CLOTHING/FABRIC PHYSICS (v2.2 note): Clothing and fabric physics are now described here in the scene field, not in @1.
EXAMPLE: 'Heavy charcoal wool coat — weighted hem with natural fall and slight swing on movement; silk lining catches light on turns.'
SETTING/ENVIRONMENT RULE: Rich visual description IS appropriate for settings and environments.
Grok generates environments from scratch — give it everything: materials, light behavior, spatial scale, surface detail, atmospheric conditions.

EXAMPLE (correct): 'A vast brutalist atrium — cracked concrete columns, industrial skylights casting shafts of cold noon light onto a polished stone floor streaked with water damage; the ceiling vaults out of frame, swallowed by shadow.'
MULTI-CHARACTER CONCURRENT MOVEMENT (hard rule): Write spatial/concurrent movement — not sequential beats.
The relationship between bodies IS the action. Everything simultaneous.

EXAMPLE (correct): 'She moves upstage through the corridor as he shifts into frame from the right, the gap between them closing, neither acknowledging the other — tension carried entirely through spatial geometry.'
EXAMPLE (wrong): 'She walks forward, then he steps in from the right, then she turns to look at him.'
NO 'and then' sequential character action chains. Concurrent action only: whilst / as / while / meanwhile.
CONCURRENT ACTION: Use 'whilst / as / while / meanwhile'. Never 'and then' — kills simultaneity.
WEIGHT & GROUNDING: Always state surface contact and weight transfer. Characters float if not grounded.
SPATIAL RELATIONS: Must be explicit — the model does not infer position.
EXAMPLE (correct): 'She crosses from DR to CC, weight shifting to her left foot as she pauses; her right hand braces against the stone pillar whilst her head turns slowly toward UL.'
EXAMPLE (wrong): 'She walks over and then she stops and then she looks around.'

blocking._DOC: 9-zone stage grid — actor's POV facing audience. NOT A–Z or any other system. Canon §6.
_DOC_grid: U=Upstage / D=Downstage / R=Right / L=Left / C=Center — ALL from actor's perspective
blocking._DOC_notation_format: Character + Action + Destination/Object  →  e.g. 'She X DR / Kn / faces UC'
_DOC_principles: LEVELS: Vary High / Medium / Low. Do not play everything standing.
TRIANGLES: Position characters in triangles not lines — straight lines flatten composition.
CHEAT OUT: Characters angle toward camera even in logically profile scenes.
MULTI-CHARACTER: Notation lists all characters simultaneously — not sequentially. Spatial relationships read as a single frozen moment extended through movement.
blocking.movement_notation: [e.g. She X CR / p / t to face UL]
blocking.composition_note: [Triangle / single / two-shot — and any cheat-out instruction]

@1._DOC: SETTING/ENVIRONMENT PLATE — the base environment image. In Grok Imagine: this IS the currently open post. Canon §2.
_DOC_rules: WHAT @1 IS: The setting/environment plate. In the Grok Imagine workflow, @1 is the page you are generating from — the current post is the base.
IN GROK IMAGINE: @1 is implicit — navigate to the setting plate post, then generate from that page. The open post IS @1.
WHAT TO DESCRIBE HERE: Atmospheric direction for the plate — energy, ambient behavior, light quality, spatial mood, sound design intent.
DIRECT IT LIKE A CHARACTER: The setting plate is a character. Give it presence, energy, behavior — not a re-description of what it shows.
CORRECT EXAMPLE: '(@1, pre-storm atmospheric pressure — the light has gone greenish and flat, the street unnaturally silent, every surface holding still.)'
CORRECT EXAMPLE: '(@1, warm golden hour — light falling at 15 degrees through western windows, dust motes suspended, surfaces amber and alive.)'
WRONG EXAMPLE: Any attempt to re-describe the full environment from scratch in this field — @1 IS the setting plate; this field carries direction only.
CLOTHING/FABRIC NOTE (v2.2): Clothing and fabric physics have moved to the scene field. @1 is no longer the clothing physics reference slot.
@1.description: [@1 atmospheric direction — energy, ambient behavior, light quality, sound design intent]

@2._DOC: CASTING REFERENCE — character identity image loaded from Grok generation history. Canon §2.
_DOC_rules: WHAT @2 IS: The casting reference. In Grok Imagine: type @ in the prompt field to reference a previously generated image from history.
ALWAYS: 'see attached render' — nothing more. Do not describe the face; Grok reads the image.
CASTING REFERENCE SOURCE: @2 is always the casting reference from History. This is the face/head reference; it provides character identity.

BACKSIDE FOLLOW SHOTS: When shooting from behind (follow cam, backside angle), use the back panel of the casting reference as @2. Grok reads the full image; the back panel provides the rear-view reference needed to maintain character consistency from behind.
DROP @2: If the subject has transformed, been destroyed, or no longer exists in original form (Canon §9).
TWO-ACTOR MODE: When both @1 and @2 are occupied by separate characters (Canon §2 / §7).

_DOC_camera: SHOT SIZE = DISTANCE. Always expressed in shot size terminology — never physical measurements. Canon §5.
ALLOWED SIZES: Wide shot / medium wide / medium shot / medium close-up / close-up / extreme close-up.
NEVER USE: '5 feet', '10 meters', etc. Physical measurements cause actors to walk out of the skybox.
'Medium wide shot, slow push-in toward her face as realization settles'
'Static locked-off wide, figure centered DC, generous negative space above'
'Handheld follow, slight shake, tracking alongside her movement through the crowd'
'Dollhouse Orbit — Establishing. Camera anchored to geometric center; slow clockwise orbit, character movement synced to orbital rate. Canon §10.'
CAMERA AS STORYTELLER: Use movement to imply action that should not be shown — zoom to reaction while physical action moves off-screen; cut to environment at moment of impact. Canon §5.

dollhouse_orbit_config._DOC: ONLY populate this block when camera type is Dollhouse Orbit / Central Anchor Infinite Orbit. Canon §10.
dollhouse_orbit_config._DOC_usage: Dollhouse Orbit = ESTABLISHING SHOTS, atmospheric sequences, title work. NOT intimacy. NOT action.
_DOC_requirements: 1. Unified 3D asset as @1 — entire environment as one model, not separate elements.
2. Church Method lock — camera anchored to exact geometric center of platform/scene.
3. Character + camera sync — character movement speed timed to match camera orbital rate.
4. Periodic plate refresh every 2–3 seconds for takes over ~10 seconds to counter drift.
5. Clean environment — simple sky, minimal moving elements reduce tracked variables.
dollhouse_orbit_config._DOC_enabled: Set true only for Dollhouse Orbit shots.
dollhouse_orbit_config.anchor_point: [Geometric center of scene — e.g. 'center of courtyard platform']
dollhouse_orbit_config.orbit_speed: [Slow / Medium — match to character movement speed]
dollhouse_orbit_config.known_limitation: Loop seam not yet clean — frame 0 and final frame do not perfectly match pose/shadow. In development.

_DOC_style: FORMAT: Cinematic style descriptor + lighting setup + atmospheric condition + director/film reference.
LIGHTING TYPES: Practical (motivated source) / Rembrandt / Split / Soft fill / Hard key / Rim / Silhouette.
Wes Anderson: 'Planimetric symmetrical composition, perfect frontal staging, vibrant controlled pastel palette, deadpan performance'
Sofia Coppola: 'Generous negative space isolating figure, soft pastel atmospheric lighting, lens flare, slow drifting camera, lingering on fabric texture'
Sean Baker: 'Naturalistic handheld, observational realism, shallow depth of field on face, background alive and chaotic'
De Palma / Giallo: 'Stylized bold tracking, lurid expressionistic lighting, subjective voyeuristic camera, dramatic zoom'
ATMOSPHERE: State physical world conditions — fog density, rain behavior, time of day, practical light sources.
EXAMPLE: 'High-contrast cinematic noir — motivated single-source practicals from practical lamp UL, deep shadow pool across DR, slight fog catch in mid-distance; Roger Deakins-adjacent.'

lighting._DOC: Dedicated lighting breakdown — supplements the style field for complex setups.
lighting.key_source: [Direction and type — e.g. 'soft window key from CL']
lighting.practicals: [Any in-scene light sources — candles, lamps, screens, fire]
lighting.color_temp: [Warm / Cool / Neutral / Mixed — specify Kelvin if exact match required]

output._DOC_sequence_length: Canon tested range: 6–10 seconds for stable one-takes. Multi-phase sequences: 10s segments (Canon §9).
output._DOC_aspect_ratio: 16:9 standard for all production output. Do not alter unless distribution spec requires otherwise.
output._DOC_resolution: Default output resolution. 480p is the generation default; upscale in post if higher output is needed.

compliance._DOC: 2257 compliance for AI actor work. All fields required when any character appears in shot. Canon-locked: all STUDIO actors are 21+ minimum.
_DOC_age_rule: AGE MUST APPEAR IN THE ACTUAL PROSE PROMPT TEXT — not just as metadata.
FORMAT: age + ethnicity + gender leads every character description in the prose.
'27 year old white woman in a charcoal wool coat...'
'36 year old Asian woman, standing at CC...'
'67 year old Black man, seated at DR...'
WRONG: 'a woman', 'young woman', 'petite girl', 'the actress' — no age = compliance failure.
VAGUE DESCRIPTORS FORBIDDEN: 'young', 'petite', 'girl', 'youthful' — these risk age ambiguity.
This is a hard legal requirement for AI-generated actor work. Not optional. No exceptions.
compliance.characters_in_shot: [YES / NO — if NO, skip remaining compliance fields]

actor_age_declaration._DOC: One entry per character in shot. Age must be 21 or older. Repeat for each character.
actor_age_declaration._DOC_declared_age: Must be integer 21 or higher. Do not use 'adult', 'mature', etc. — use the actual number.
actor_age_declaration._DOC_age_in_prose_confirmed: Set true only after verifying age + ethnicity + gender appear verbatim at the START of this character's description in prose_prompt.

compliance.compliance_note: [Director confirmation: 'All characters declared 21+; age/ethnicity/gender explicit in prose.' Or: 'No characters in shot — compliance N/A.']
compliance._DOC_no_ambiguous: Set true after scanning prose_prompt for: 'young', 'petite', 'girl', 'youthful', 'teenage', 'small', 'child-like' — if any present, rewrite before generation.

prose_prompt: [THE ACTUAL GROK SUBMISSION TEXT — see rules below]
_DOC_prose_prompt: THIS IS THE FIELD SUBMITTED TO GROK. All other fields above are planning/documentation.
PROSE RATIO RULE (Canon §11): 30–50% cinematic language woven into narrative description.
Too many technical terms → mechanical output, loses story.
Too few → loses camera control.
Target: roughly 1 cinematic instruction per 2 narrative sentences.
HYBRID FORMULA: Subject + Action/Movement + Scene/Environment + Cinematic Language + Lighting/Atmosphere + Style Reference
SENTENCE STRUCTURE = PACING (Canon §11):
Short sentences / fragments → urgency, impact, fast cuts
Long flowing sentences → immersion, reflection, slow scenes
Match rhythm to the scene's emotional register.
Read prompts aloud — aim for musical flow.
PARAGRAPH BREAKS = SHOT RHYTHM:
New paragraph = suggested shot change or significant beat shift.
Use white space to pace the model the way an editor paces a cut.
WORKFLOW (Canon §11):
1. Start with narrative core — what happens, why it matters.
2. Layer cinematic grammar — shot type, camera move, framing.
3. Add style/director reference.
4. Control pacing through sentence and paragraph length.
5. Test variants: prose-heavy / technical-heavy / well-blended.
CONCURRENT ACTION: whilst / as / while / meanwhile. Never 'and then'.
NEGATIVE SPACE: State it explicitly — 'generous negative space above and to frame left'.
DEPTH PLANES: Foreground / midground / background layering increases dimensionality.

narrative_context._DOC: Production planning fields — not submitted to Grok. For script integration and continuity.
narrative_context.narrative_beat: [What this shot accomplishes in the story — one sentence]
narrative_context.emotional_register: [What the audience should feel — e.g. 'mounting dread', 'quiet longing', 'wonder']
narrative_context.preceding_shot: [Shot ID that comes before — for continuity reference]
narrative_context.following_shot: [Shot ID that follows — for cut point planning]
narrative_context.sequence_role: [Establishing / Character entrance / Reaction / Transition / Climax / Resolution]
narrative_context.continuity_notes: [Costume state, hair position, prop placement, lighting that must match across cuts]

production_workflow._DOC: Canon §12 workflow stages. Check off as completed.
production_workflow._DOC_elf_pass: Major pictures / flow established with simple blocking notation.
production_workflow._DOC_scene_phase: Full scene built with stage directions and blocking.
production_workflow._DOC_video_prompt_phase: Scene translated into this JSON prompt architecture.
production_workflow._DOC_legal_gate: GREEN = proceed. RED = hard stop. No debate. No exceptions.

multi_phase_config._DOC: For long-form sequences 30s+. Break into 10-second segments. Canon §9.
multi_phase_config._DOC_enabled: Set enabled: true and populate phases only for sequences requiring multi-phase.

phases._DOC: Load @1 (setting plate) and @2 (casting reference) if subject exists in original form.
Drop @2 if subject has transformed. Maintain @1 for continuity.
Camera can shift — push-in, slow zoom — to signal conclusion.

multi_phase_config.physics_note: Asymmetrical and chaotic structure consistently outperforms perfect radial symmetry for physics realism.

validation_rail._DOC: Pre-generation checklist. Verify all before submitting prose_prompt to Grok.

@1_is_setting_plate_direction_only.rule: @1 carries atmospheric direction for the setting plate ONLY — not a re-description of the environment, not clothing/fabric physics (v2.2)

@2_single_phrase_only.rule: @2 is exactly 'see attached render' — nothing more, nothing less

@2_casting_reference_correct.rule: @2 source is always the casting reference from History. Backside shots: use back panel of casting reference.

character_behavioral_direction_only.rule: Characters in prose: behavioral direction ONLY (action, movement, energy, intention) — NO appearance description (face, hair, skin, body). Editor has 3D render; Grok has casting reference.

setting_description_present.rule: Settings and environments: rich visual description provided — materials, light behavior, spatial scale, surface detail, atmosphere. Grok generates these from scratch.

multi_character_concurrent_not_sequentia.rule: Multi-character scenes: spatial/concurrent movement only — no sequential action beats. No 'then he', 'then she', 'and then' chains. The gap between bodies IS the action.

blocking_uses_9zone_grid.rule: Zone notation is UR/UC/UL / CR/CC/CL / DR/DC/DL only — not A–Z or any other system

dollhouse_orbit_is_establishing_not_inti.rule: If Dollhouse Orbit used: scene type is establishing/atmospheric/title — not intimate or action

prose_ratio_30_to_50_percent.rule: Cinematic language occupies 30–50% of prose_prompt — not under, not over

no_and_then_sequencing.rule: Concurrent action uses whilst/as/while/meanwhile — no 'and then' anywhere in prose

camera_field_no_physical_measurements.rule: Camera distance expressed in shot size terms only — no meters, feet, inches

scene_field_no_camera_language.rule: Scene field contains stage direction only — camera language belongs in the camera field

compliance_2257_age_ethnicity_gender_in_.rule: 2257: age + ethnicity + gender appear DIRECTLY IN PROSE TEXT for every character — not just in compliance metadata. Format: '[age] year old [ethnicity] [gender]' leads each character description.

legal_gate_0_green.rule: Legal Gate 0 cleared GREEN before any generation attempt

nudity_not_on_frame_1.rule: If any clothing state change intended: character baked in from frame 1, not cold-started nude

_FILING_NOTE: Save completed instances to Pipeline/Video_Prompts/ with naming: [ProjectCode]_[SceneID]_[ShotID]_v[N]_[YYYYMMDD].json
_CANON_REF: STUDIO_Production_Canon_v1.0.md — locked reference. All deviations require Director sign-off and new Canon version.
